stimulus: British astronomers Cecilia Payne and Henry Norris Russell are often credited with discovering that the Sun is composed mostly of hydrogen, but this account is incomplete. ______blank work depended on years of spectroscopic measurements made by earlier researchers, especially Annie Jump Cannon, who classified hundreds of thousands of stellar spectra.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The convention being tested is the use of possessive determiners. The plural possessive determiner “their” correctly refers to the plural conjoined noun phrase “Payne and Russell.”

Choice A is the contraction “it is.” Choice B is a singular possessive determiner that doesn’t agree with the plural antecedent. Choice C is the contraction “they are,” not a possessive.
